// kernel.architecture.inspect()

Kernel Architecture: Unveiling the Operating System's Core

The Kernel: The Heart of the OS

The kernel is the core program managing hardware, processes, and system resources — the privileged bridge between software and silicon.

Managing the Machine

Coordinating hardware, processes, memory, SMP, and system resources under a single supervisory authority.

Kernel Architecture Overview

User Space
Applications Libraries …
System Calls

User app invokes a syscall → flow travels through I/O, memory allocation

System Call Interface
System Processes System daemons, services, schedulers, etc.
System Interfaces
Kernel Space
fs File System
Module
vm Virtual Memory
Module
io I/O Manager &
Device Drivers
Hardware
CPU
RAM
Disk
Devices

Process Scheduler

Round Robin
Ready Queue IO Wait
P1P2P3P4
CPU
P1P2P3P4
Timeline

System Call Execution Flow

  1. 1 Syscall trap → kernel mode
  2. 2 Data movement sys_open()
  3. 3 Data movement sys_read()
  4. 4 Data movement sys_write()
  5. 5 Data movement sys_close()
  6. 6 Return → user space

Interrupt Handling

Hardware Interrupt ISR Kernel

Animation · IRQ → vector dispatch → handler
